title: SDUSD Teacher Retention & CBS Report
text: This report summarizes teacher retention rate, teacher shifting rate, and Capacity-Building Strategy (CBS) scores for 35 schools across the 2020-21 through 2024-25 academic years. Use the Overall, By Cohort, and By School sections below to explore the trend plots, and the Correlation Analysis section for how retention/shifting relate to CBS strategies and other APR variables.

title: SDUSD Teacher Retention & CBS Report
text:
Correlation Analysis
How retention rate and shifting rate relate to the 5 CBS strategy scores and other numeric APR/participation variables. See 04-correlation-analyses.R for the underlying methodology.

Correlation Matrix

PearsonSpearman

























Full correlation table (Pearson and Spearman, every predictor x every outcome):








Scatterplots
One panel per predictor variable, with a linear trend line.

Retention RateShifting Rate



























High vs. Low CBS Score Comparison
High = CBS score of 3 (top of the 1-3 maturity scale); Low = score of 1-2.

Retention RateShifting Rate

























Test results (t-test and Wilcoxon rank-sum, High vs. Low):








Cohort Comparisons (ANOVA)

CCSPP - Retention RateCCSPP - Shifting RateSDUSD - Retention RateSDUSD - Shifting Rate















































ANOVA results:






Group means by cohort:








Lagged Correlation
Spearman correlation of this year’s CBS score against the same-year outcome (“lag 0”) vs. next-year outcome (“lag 1”), within school.
